sanskrit: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः । जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
hindiTranslation: समतायुक्त मनीषी साधक कर्मजन्य फलका त्याग करके जन्मरूप बन्धनसे मुक्त होकर निर्विकार पदको प्राप्त हो जाते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Wise people, united in clarity, abandon the fruit born of action and reach the flawless state, free from the bondage of birth.
essence: Freedom comes when action is done without clutching its reward.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Wise people let go of the results of their actions. That freedom breaks the chain that keeps them bound to repeated birth and suffering.
simpleInsight: You can do the work and stop clutching the reward. That loosening brings real relief.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna keeps teaching the same turning point: act with clear understanding, but do not cling to what the action produces. That release leads beyond bondage.
modernRelevance: You send the hard email, then keep checking for a reply every five minutes. The action is complete, but your mind keeps demanding ownership of the result. This verse cuts that cord.
translationLiteral: कर्मजं (born of action) / बुद्धियुक्ताः (those united with discerning clarity) / हि (indeed) / फलम् (fruit) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / मनीषिणः (wise people) / जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः (freed from the bondage of birth) / पदम् (state) / गच्छन्ति (they go) / अनामयम् (flawless, free from affliction)
